1	use super::{ExprLinter, Lint, LintKind};
2	use crate::expr::Expr;
3	use crate::expr::FixedPhrase;
4	use crate::expr::LongestMatchOf;
5	use crate::expr::SimilarToPhrase;
6	use crate::linting::Suggestion;
7	use crate::linting::expr_linter::Chunk;
8	use crate::weir::weir_expr_to_expr;
9	use crate::{Token, TokenStringExt};
10	
11	pub struct MapPhraseLinter {
12	    description: String,
13	    expr: Box<dyn Expr>,
14	    correct_forms: Vec<String>,
15	    message: String,
16	    lint_kind: LintKind,
17	}
18	
19	impl MapPhraseLinter {
20	    pub fn new(
21	        expr: Box<dyn Expr>,
22	        correct_forms: impl IntoIterator<Item = impl ToString>,
23	        message: impl ToString,
24	        description: impl ToString,
25	        lint_kind: Option<LintKind>,
26	    ) -> Self {
27	        Self {
28	            description: description.to_string(),
29	            expr,
30	            correct_forms: correct_forms.into_iter().map(|f| f.to_string()).collect(),
31	            message: message.to_string(),
32	            lint_kind: lint_kind.unwrap_or(LintKind::Miscellaneous),
33	        }
34	    }
35	
36	    pub fn new_similar_to_phrase(phrase: &'static str, detectable_distance: u8) -> Self {
37	        Self::new(
38	            Box::new(SimilarToPhrase::from_phrase(phrase, detectable_distance)),
39	            [phrase],
40	            format!("Did you mean the phrase `{phrase}`?"),
41	            format!("Looks for slight improper modifications to the phrase `{phrase}`."),
42	            None,
43	        )
44	    }
45	
46	    pub fn new_fixed_phrases(
47	        phrase: impl IntoIterator<Item = impl AsRef<str>>,
48	        correct_forms: impl IntoIterator<Item = impl ToString>,
49	        message: impl ToString,
50	        description: impl ToString,
51	        lint_kind: Option<LintKind>,
52	    ) -> Self {
53	        let patterns = LongestMatchOf::new(
54	            phrase
55	                .into_iter()
56	                .map(|p| {
57	                    let expr: Box<dyn Expr> = Box::new(weir_expr_to_expr(p.as_ref()).unwrap());
58	                    expr
59	                })
60	                .collect(),
61	        );
62	
63	        Self::new(
64	            Box::new(patterns),
65	            correct_forms,
66	            message,
67	            description,
68	            lint_kind,
69	        )
70	    }
71	
72	    pub fn new_fixed_phrase(
73	        phrase: impl AsRef<str>,
74	        correct_forms: impl IntoIterator<Item = impl ToString>,
75	        message: impl ToString,
76	        description: impl ToString,
77	        lint_kind: Option<LintKind>,
78	    ) -> Self {
79	        Self::new(
80	            Box::new(FixedPhrase::from_phrase(phrase.as_ref())),
81	            correct_forms,
82	            message,
83	            description,
84	            lint_kind,
85	        )
86	    }
87	
88	    pub fn new_closed_compound(phrase: impl AsRef<str>, correct_form: impl ToString) -> Self {
89	        let message = format!(
90	            "Did you mean the closed compound `{}`?",
91	            correct_form.to_string()
92	        );
93	
94	        let description = format!(
95	            "Looks for incorrect spacing inside the closed compound `{}`.",
96	            correct_form.to_string()
97	        );
98	
99	        Self::new_fixed_phrase(
100	            phrase,
101	            [correct_form],
102	            message,
103	            description,
104	            Some(LintKind::Miscellaneous),
105	        )
106	    }
107	}

109	impl ExprLinter for MapPhraseLinter {
110	    type Unit = Chunk;
111	
112	    fn expr(&self) -> &dyn Expr {
113	        self.expr.as_ref()
114	    }
115	
116	    fn match_to_lint(&self, matched_tokens: &[Token], source: &[char]) -> Option<Lint> {
117	        let span = matched_tokens.span()?;
118	        let matched_text = span.get_content(source);
119	
120	        Some(Lint {
121	            span,
122	            lint_kind: self.lint_kind,
123	            suggestions: self
124	                .correct_forms
125	                .iter()
126	                .map(|correct_form| {
127	                    Suggestion::replace_with_match_case(
128	                        correct_form.chars().collect(),
129	                        matched_text,
130	                    )
131	                })
132	                .collect(),
133	            message: self.message.to_string(),
134	            priority: 31,
135	        })
136	    }
137	
138	    fn description(&self) -> &str {
139	        self.description.as_str()
140	    }
141	}

The bug fix is complete. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Summary

**Bug**: Users commonly confuse "flaunt" (to show off / be ostentatious) with "flout" (to openly ignore rules). The tool had no rule to detect and correct this misuse.

**Fix**: Created a new lint rule `FlauntFlout` that detects when "flaunt" (and its inflections: flaunted, flaunts, flaunting) is used in the context of rules/laws and suggests the correct "flout" form.

### Files changed:

1. **`harper-core/src/linting/flaunt_flout.rs`** (new file) — The lint implementation:
   - Matches forms of "flaunt" (`flaunt`, `flaunted`, `flaunts`, `flaunting`) followed by any number of intermediate words, then a rule/law word (`rule`, `rules`, `law`, `laws`, `regulation`, `regulations`)
   - Suggests the corresponding form of "flout" (`flout`, `flouted`, `flouts`, `flouting`) with proper case matching
   - Uses `LintKind::Malapropism` and provides a descriptive message explaining the difference

2. **`harper-core/src/linting/mod.rs`** — Added `mod flaunt_flout;` declaration in alphabetical order

3. **`harper-core/src/linting/lint_group.rs`** — Added both the `use` import and the `insert_expr_rule!(FlauntFlout, true);` registration in alphabetical order

All existing tests (4683 tests) continue to pass with 0 failures.
